jobs.title: Software Quality Assurance Lead
jobs.description: This is a fantastic opportunity to establish a new capability within our Digital Products team. You will define standards and processes as well as help us to understand best practice and innovative approaches to quality, which ensure our customers have a great experience using our digital products every time. You will work with teams across our business to improve UAT, providing them with foundational tools and concepts so they are able to provide useful, thorough testing to an appropriate level. This is a strategic role which may have an element of hands on QA initially. At apetito, we are committed to providing high-quality products and services to our customers. From the recipes our chefs create to the digital platforms our customers use, we want to deliver the best to make a real difference to our customers. To help us bring this to life we are looking for an enthusiastic and experienced Quality Assurance (QA) Lead to join our growing team.

jobs.title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
jobs.description: This position is working 16 hours per week, any 2 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. View our latest TV ad here: https://www.wiltshirefarmfoods.com/blog/our-new-tv-ad-launches £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

jobs.title: Customer Service Advisor
jobs.description: This role is working 15 hours per week, any 2 in 6 days The role of our Customer Services Advisor is critical to the success of our business. Our customers are predominantly elderly and rely upon our office teams to take their orders over the phone or via the internet. The defining characteristics of our Customer Services Advisors are that they’re organised and efficient. However, apart from the technical skills that are required with the role, the most important attributes are that we demonstrate empathy, patience, kindness, friendliness, care and warmth to all our customers. The role involves the taking of customer orders and also the making of outbound courtesy calls. These calls are particularly important as many customers live alone and the phone calls they receive are often the only contact they may have in a single day. As such, many customers become very attached to their “friends” at Wiltshire Farm Foods. Wiltshire Farm Foods supplies frozen meal solutions direct to the consumer. We have a cold store to accommodate the stock necessary to be able to be able to deliver to our customers each week. The ultimate goal is to provide a service to our customers that exceed expectations while at the same time continuously improving efficiencies. £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

jobs.title: Ingénieur Sécurité Cloud GCP ou AWS F/H
jobs.description: Dans le cadre de la mise en place d’un Centre de Compétences « Cloud Center of Excellence » pour notre client, nous recherchons un(e) Ingénieur Sécurité Cloud. Vous serez amenés à intervenir sur les sujets suivants : - Accompagner les équipes informatiques dans les pratiques de « sécurité dès la conception », visant à mettre en œuvre des politiques et des modèles de sécurité tout au long du cycle de vie des applications, de la conception à l'exécution • Maîtriser et mettre en place les principes du « Zero Trust Architecture ». • Maîtriser les services AWS ou GCP relatifs à la sécurité. • Garantir la prise de décisions de sécurité judicieuses, conformément aux politiques de l'entreprise. • Etre certifié o AWS Security speciality o ou GCP Security Engineer Ce poste situé à Guyancourt (78) Chez Orange, seules vos compétences comptent : Quel que soit votre âge, genre, origine, religion, orientation sexuelle, neuroatypie, handicap ou apparence, nous encourageons la diversité au sein de nos équipes car c’est une force pour le collectif et un vecteur d’innovation Nous sommes une entreprise handi-accueillante : n'hésitez pas à nous faire part de vos besoins spécifiques Orange obtient la certification Top Employer Global 2021. Cette certification consacre les meilleures politiques et pratiques en termes de programmes de ressources humaines. Le Groupe est certifié « Top Employer » depuis maintenant 12 ans.

jobs.title: Business Manager Performance Energétique F/H
jobs.description: Notre entité, intégrée au sein d’Orange Business, dispose de compétences pointues et reconnues dans l'intégration de solutions globales en Connectivité (LAN/WIFI/WAN/SDWAN/Data Center/MPN), Workplace (O365/Teams, Cloud Telephony, Digital Building, Mobilité) & IoT auprès de clients grands comptes et entreprises. Son rôle est de simplifier la vie digitale de ses clients, en leur offrant les meilleures expériences collaboratives tout en garantissant la meilleure connectivité grâce à des offres sur mesure. La Practice Performance Energétique et Développement Durable a pour mission de développer des offres de services et de technologies destinées à optimiser les consommations d’énergie et réduire l’empreinte carbone de ses clients. Missions : Les missions confiées au Business Manager sont organisées autour de trois grands axes : les actions commerciales, l’expertise et l’animation marketing. Définir et déployer les actions commerciales Identifier des cibles clients prioritaires et établir des listes de prospects Définir les méthodes et les objectifs des plans de prospection Contribuer activement à ces plans par des actions de prospection directe Identifier et animer les relais commerciaux internes (KAM, AM, BM, etc.) Alimenter et mettre à jour les outils de suivi commercial (CRM, fichier de suivi pipe) et assurer le reporting auprès de la direction et des parties prenantes Développer et porter l’expertise métier Apporter l’expertise métier tant en interne qu’auprès des prospects et clients Préparer et participer aux rendez-vous organisés par les commerciaux pour aider à qualifier le besoin et les réponses adéquates Assurer une veille globale (concurrence, marché, réglementation, acteurs, etc.) Détection d'opportunité de nouvelles solutions à intégrer dans le portefeuille de solutions ou partenariats à développer Définir les actions marketing et appuyer leur réalisation Appui à la réalisation d'outils de communication (supports marketing et commercial, références clients, campagne e-mailing, etc.) Proposition et appui à la réalisation de campagnes marketing opérationnel Aide à la préparation et participation aux salons d'affaires, webinaires et évènements divers Le Business Manager est assisté dans sa mission par 2 alternants qui l’appuient sur les plans d’action commerciaux et marketing.

jobs.title: Manufacturing Team Leader
jobs.description: Team Leader Sweets department- PM shift 40 hours per week, 14:00 - 22:00 Monday to Friday. Your role as Team Leader within our Sweets Department will be delivering plan completion; controlling costs, ensuring that KPI targets are achieved, coming up with continuous improvement ideas and implementation. You will ensure that the department runs to its maximum efficiency with right first time quality productivity. The department is keen to promote excellent team work by employing people who have a positive (can do) attitude. By achieving this, the department is in the best position to produce products in an efficient manner. We aim to not only achieve our targets but improve on them as well. Your role will be to look after the Sweets Kitchen starting the process on making the mix's and cooking the products, The Production Lines throughout sweets ensuring the lines are getting the materials they need and dealing with escalations that arise. Taking ownership of the department and the wash-up ensuring it is always left in the best position it can be. Sweets Produce around 330,000 units a week of finished product but make around 500,000 units a week when you include all the WIP (work in progress) tasks which makes it unique within Apetio Trowbridge. You will manage a team of 15+ with the skill sets to achieve tasks, training and upskilling is a must for the team success. £18.19 per hour + shift allowance + bonus + benefits

jobs.title: IT Support Engineer
jobs.description: Are you looking to start your career in IT? Or maybe you have some experience of first-line IT support and are looking for a new opportunity with a great employer? We are looking for a first line IT Support Engineer to be part of our team as we undertake a significant transformation to modernize our IT services across the apetito group. Our team is committed to delivering world-class services to the business and supporting its continued growth so you'll need to bring customer service skills and a passion for development. This role will play a pivotal part of our service delivery offering, being a first point of contact for our end users and therefore being the face and first impression of the IT service desk experience. apetito business operates 24/7 providing a vital service to our customers. IT is central to the success of the business and the IT team supports the infrastructure and services vital to its operation. We are embarking on a period of exciting change so this is a great oportunity for someone who is looking to grow with us. Why Join Us? Be part of an innovative and growing food manufacturing company with a commitment to sustainability and quality. Lead transformative initiatives and make a direct impact on operational success. Work in a collaborative environment that values diversity and fosters professional growth. Be part of a company that values the individual and has an “employee first” mindset. This role is based out of our Head offices in Trowbridge, Wiltshire – With a requirement to be on-site five days a week. You will be part of an on-call rota for out-of-hours support. Remunerated with an on-call payment for active on-call weeks. Up to £30,000 per annum + Bonus + Benefits (depending on experience).

jobs.title: Client Development Manager
jobs.description: Our Client Development Managers (CDMs) are a key link between our customers and apetito. Customers see their CDM as the face of apetito – they are an instrumental part of the success of apetito partnerships A successful CDM will be an authentic, enthusiastic self-starter, who can develop long-term partnerships with customers and achieve commercial targets including high levels of customer retention and loyalty. Our CDMs manage their own customers whilst having a team of apetito colleagues to support them. This is an important role and gives us a real point of difference in the market. The Care Homes Team are: Dedicated experts in the provision of food for older people We are genuine partners to Care Homes and their teams We are proud to create food that is enjoyed and shared. Our Care Homes team have a very forward thinking culture that will really help us to accelerate growth over the next 5 years. We have a mix of experienced managers, food professionals and graduates who are positive and passionate about what we do. We make a real difference. Click here to hear from a CDM in our carehomes team about the role. This role is covering the North West of England and will require travel across the region as well as to our head office in Trowbridge, Wiltshire. A company car is provided so you'll need a full UK driving licence, ideally with no points or endorsements.

jobs.title: Office Supervisor
jobs.description: The new Office Supervisor, for our Wiltshire Farm Foods territory covering Perth & Kinross, must be passionate and have the energy and commitment to support the Retail Sales and Operations Manager to create an exemplar of outstanding performance in customer service and sales. As Office Supervisor you'll supervise the office based team to deliver exceptional service to our customers via phone and and email. Customer service and empathy for the needs of our predominately elderly customers is at the heart of what we do and you'll need to be part of living those values. The Wiltshire Farm Foods network is actively supported with national marketing, including television, national press and direct mail marketing campaigns. You can also tap into our support from our Wiltshire Head Office. £14.94 per hour + bonus + benefits

jobs.title: PR Executive
jobs.description: At apetito | Wiltshire Farm Foods, we are on an ambitious journey. With bold growth targets ahead, building trust and telling our story well will be key to our success. As PR Executive, you will play an important role in delivering day-to-day PR activity across the business, helping to bring our stories to life and support our reputation across priority markets. This is a hands-on role that offers excellent exposure, responsibility and development for someone looking to grow their career in PR within a purpose-led organisation. Working closely with the PR Manager and Senior PR Executive, you will take responsibility for defined PR activity, supporting press office delivery, media engagement and content creation, while building confidence, capability and sector understanding over time. You'll have some experience in PR, communications (or a related field) and be skilled in writing and producing content for different audiences. Strong organisation skills will be critical, as will as aptitude for communication and storytelling. Above all, you will embody passion to help share how apetito | Wiltshire Farm Foods are making a real difference every day.

jobs.title: Production Operator - Sweets
jobs.description: The Sweets Department are keen to promote excellent team work by employing people who have a positive attitude, who are prepared to learn a number of skills so that they are able to work in various positions within the department. By achieving this, the department is in the best position to produce quality products, in a safe environment in an efficient manner. We aim to not only achieve our targets but improve on them as well. We aim to make around 300,000 units a week of sellable stock and in excess of 500,000 units of work in progress stock, you will be part of a team that is driven to hit targets and also have the product right first time. The working hours for this role are Sunday to Thursday 2200 - 0600 and is based at our UK Headquartes in Trowbridge Wiltshire .

jobs.title: ALTERNANCE - Technicien Modalités F/H
jobs.description: Vos missions : Assurer les interventions techniques préventives et curatives sur nos DM Maintenances curatives : recherche de panne sur équipement / diagnostic, remplacement de pièces détachées sur les équipements de type suivants : table de radiologie (tube, accessoires…), mammographe (tube, capteur, système de compression, système de refroidissement…), panoramique dentaire (générateur, carte électronique, faisceau de câble, serre tempe), Conebeam dentaire (générateur, tube, carte micro), ostéodensitométrie (capteur). Purge et installation de logiciels sur les consoles dédiées, préparation de nouveaux postes informatiques Maintenances préventives : révision mécanique (graissage, dépoussiérage), étalonnage et calibration de capteurs. Back up de configuration informatique, mise à jour logiciel. Installation d’équipement de radiologie : panoramiques dentaire et conebeam principalement. Fixation murale et au sol de l’équipement, assemblage installation et paramétrage. Configuration informatique pour environnement du Client.

jobs.title: Alternant Chef de Produit Voice IA F/H
jobs.description: 🟨 Nous recherchons un(e) alternant(e) pour renforcer l'équipe marketing IA. 🟨Vos missions seront les suivantes : Analyse du marché et de la concurrence - Étudier les solutions existantes de bots et leurs fonctionnalités - Construction de battlecard - Maintien de benchmark sur les solutions internes Participation à la construction du produit et animation du produit - Support à la construction d'expressions de besoin - Suivi des évolutions fonctionnelles et pilotage agile - Construction d'outils d'aide à la vente - Accompagnement des vendeurs - Participation à la construction des plans de communication internes et externes - Suivi de projets - Suivi de KPIs Participation à la création de maquettes ou prototypes - Effectuer des tests utilisateurs et recueillir des retours. - Rédaction et optimisation des prompts - Analyser les scénarios d’usage - Test des prompts en situation simulée

jobs.title: Administrateur système Linux F/H
jobs.description: En mission interne, vous interviendrez dans une unité d’élite appelée « Solutions Cockpit » au sein de la direction « Trust » d'Orange Business Services. Ce département a pour vocation d’être un fournisseur de solutions de « confiance » pour répondre à l’exploitation des solutions Réseau, IT, Cloud & Sécurité de nos clients « très sensibles » Ce département, d’une soixantaine de personnes rassemblées autour d’une même organisation, localisé sur un seul site, est optimisé pour répondre aux besoins de réactivité et de sécurité de nos clients. Il permet d’offrir une exploitation sécurisée répondant à des exigences spécifiques contractuelles ou réglementaires (mise en place de mesures spécifiques de sécurité). Votre rôle : Votre mission sera de répondre aux besoins des clients « Trust » du nouveau segment client « Défense & Sécurité », via la mise en place d’outils nécessaires aux différents métiers de la direction (équipes SAV, RSSI, d’experts produits et de production). Dans un collectif de 7 administrateurs au sein de l’équipe « HALO » (Hosting & Administration Linux Operations), vous aurez en charge l’administration des serveurs Linux permettant d’héberger les différents outils d’administration, de les maintenir à jour, de les faire évoluer, et de contribuer à la satisfaction des utilisateurs de ces services. Pour cela vos missions consisteront à : Accompagner les métiers pour collecter leurs besoins ; Créer les maquettes correspondantes aux besoins remontés ; Concevoir et intégrer des solutions techniques à partir d’outils existants sur le marché, l’open source est mis en avant ; Présenter les solutions techniques aux équipes qui ont fourni le cahier des charges ; Tester les outils en phase de préproduction (déploiement via Ansible) ; Maintenir et intégrer des outils de déploiement (CI/CD, Ansible, Gitlab, Jenkins, etc…) ; Rédiger la documentation associée ; Détecter, analyser et corriger les dysfonctionnements, bugs ou autres, liés aux outils développés et installés ; Respecter les bonnes pratiques de sécurité (durcissements, préconisations ANSSI, élévation de privilèges etc.). Exemple de projet : création d’un espace de stockage partagé intra et inter équipes, déploiement de l’outil sur les différents projets, maintien en condition opérationnelle et de sécurité de celui-ci et de la plateforme le supportant. Vous intervenez donc dans les phases de think, de build et de run, ce qui rend le poste riche et qui permet de suivre un projet dans sa globalité.

jobs.title: Analyste Développeur Senior F/H
jobs.description: Description du poste 🟨 Nous recrutons un Analyste développeur , pour intervenir auprès de notre client du secteur Banque Assurance. Vous participerez à la conception, au développement et à la maintenance de nos applications, tout en assurant une gestion efficace des incidents en environnement RUN. Le projet est actuellement en régie, ce qui permet une flexibilité optimale pour répondre aux besoins spécifiques et ajuster les interventions en fonction de l'évolution du projet. 🟨 Vos missions seront les suivant e s : Concevoir et développer des applications en Java et Node.js Travailler sur la gestion et l'optimisation des bases de données Oracle SQL Réaliser des tests et déployer des solutions en intégrant les processus CI/CD (Jenkins, Docker) Assurer la gestion des incidents en environnement RUN Participer aux cérémonies Agile SCRUM et contribuer à l'amélioration continue des processus

jobs.title: Alternance - Chargé(e) de recrutement F/H
jobs.description: Vous intégrerez une équipe de trois chargé(e)s de recrutement dans un environnement de travail dynamique et stimulant. Vous serez responsable de la campagne de stages et d’alternance et assurerez l’ensemble du processus de recrutement pour des profils variés dans les domaines de l’IT, des réseaux, de la data/IA, de la cybersécurité et du cloud. Vos missions seront les suivantes : Recueil des besoins et brief avec les managers Sourcing & chasse Marketing et publication des offres d’emploi Pré-sélection des profils sur CV Pré-qualification et entretien en distanciel/physique Rédaction de comptes-rendus auprès des managers Suivi du processus de recrutement (contrat, convention, reporting…) Participation à des évènements de recrutement (forums, job dating…) Vous participerez également à des événements de recrutement et d’attractivité employeur : Participation physique ou en virtuel à des salons, forums, jobdatings Promotion des événements sur les réseaux sociaux et autres supports

jobs.title: Digital Marketing Manager
jobs.description: Fixed term contract for 12 months Join our rapidly growing business in a role as Digital Marketing Manager (FTC) focusing primarily on the digital marketing and paid digital strategies to support B2B’s commercial growth, as well as managing the apetito.co.uk website strategy. As a digital marketing specialist, your role will be pivotal in enhancing our online presence. Reporting to the Head of Proposition & Marketing, you will play a key role in contributing to the company’s ambitious growth targets and driving our online brand visibility. This is a fantastic opportunity for a collaborative, analytical and creative marketeer to work within a supportive, experienced and passionate team. This role is based in Trowbridge and you will be required to work on-site at least 3 days per week.

jobs.title: Technicien de production H/F
jobs.description: Vous êtes passionné(e) par la cybersécurité et souhaitez évoluer dans un environnement innovant en pleine expansion ? En mission interne et dans le cadre de l'expansion du Global Security Operations Center (GSOC) du groupe Orange, vous interviendrez au sein de la Direction de la Sécurité (DSEC) pour piloter des projets d'intégration de solutions de cybersécurité. Votre rôle sera essentiel pour assurer la supervision et la protection des actifs critiques du groupe. Vos missions : Piloter des projets d’intégration de solutions de cybersécurité : suivre le déploiement des solutions de sécurité et/ou mise en place de la supervision. Collaborer avec des équipes pluridisciplinaires : travailler main dans la main avec nos experts techniques, d'autres entités du groupe, équipes d’intégration et d’exploitation ainsi que celles du GSOC pour garantir la mise en œuvre des solutions adaptées. Évaluer et synthétiser les besoins : recueillir les besoins et synthétiser l'information, rédiger des comptes-rendus, élaborer des plannings, préparer des présentations pour le comité de pilotage, réaliser des schémas d’architecture haut niveau.

jobs.title: Ingénieur Support Sécurité H/F
jobs.description: Orange International Networks Infrastructures & Services (OINIS) est une direction technique du Groupe Orange, déployée à l’échelle mondiale avec plus de 1400 collaborateurs hautement qualifiés. Notre mission est de concevoir, planifier, maintenir et sécuriser les réseaux internationaux et services associés pour l’ensemble du groupe. Créée en 2021, OINIS SEC a pour objectif de protéger l’ensemble des infrastructures d’OINIS, depuis l’ingénierie des solutions jusqu’à la supervision de sécurité, en passant par le déploiement. Elle se structure autour de deux pôles principaux : expertise/anticipation et développement/support. En intégrant l’équipe ENG (Engineering), composée de 12 collaborateurs répartis en France, Maroc et Inde, vos missions principales seont les suivantes : Définition, l’industrialisation et le support des outils de sécurité (contrôle d’accès, authentification, gestion des vulnérabilités, supervision). Déploiement et le maintien en conditions opérationnelles et de sécurité des équipements réseaux. Mise en place des règles de filtrage Firewall. Conception d’architectures et solutions pour la protection des infrastructures, en comprenant les besoins clients et en sélectionnant les composants adaptés. Collaboration étroite avec les équipes Network et TelcoCloud pour le déploiement et la gestion des solutions. Promotion des solutions open source, tout en utilisant des produits commerciaux si nécessaire. Rédaction de documents d’architecture, guides de bonnes pratiques et référentiels. Production, configuration et déploiement des équipements réseaux IP. Support aux équipes opérationnelles pour la gestion et la résolution des incidents. Intégration de l’automatisation dans le déploiement des solutions de sécurité. Gestion de projets pour déployer les solutions dans le respect des plannings et contraintes financières.

jobs.title: Expert IoT et Réseaux H/F
jobs.description: Rejoignez une équipe dynamique au sein d'Orange, leader dans le domaine des technologies de communication. Nous sommes engagés à offrir des solutions innovantes et responsables qui répondent aux besoins de nos clients dans un monde en constante évolution. Notre culture d'entreprise valorise la collaboration, la créativité et l'exploration, tout en plaçant l'humain au cœur de nos préoccupations. Prêt(e) à connecter le futur ? Rejoignez-nous et devenez l'architecte des solutions IoT ! Description des missions : Vous concevrez et développerez des solutions IoT innovantes en intégrant des technologies réseau avancées. Vous intégrerez des modules logiciels de partenaires et fournisseurs tiers pour enrichir nos solutions. Vous rédigerez des spécifications techniques et fonctionnelles claires et précises pour guider le développement et/ou réaliserez des mission de conseil ou d’audit auprès de nos clients. Vous contribuerez au développement de bout en bout d’applications (front et back) et à leur test déploiement chez nos clients industriels. Vous accompagnerez nos clients dans la validation métier de la solution, en veillant à son bon fonctionnement dans l'environnement de production. Vous assurerez le suivi et l'optimisation des performances des solutions déployées sur le terrain. Vous participerez à des études et projets d'innovation pour anticiper les évolutions du marché et des technologies. Vous serez en charge de la formation et du support technique pour les équipes internes et les clients. Vous collaborerez avec des équipes pluridisciplinaires pour définir et mettre en œuvre des architectures IoT robustes. Vous apporterez votre expertise en support à l'avant-vente pour aider à la définition des besoins clients et à la proposition de solutions adaptées.

jobs.title: Ingénieur Devops
jobs.description: Intégré à une équipe de 21 personnes composés d'ingénieurs DevOps, Ingénieurs SecOps, Administrateurs Systèmes et de Chefs de projet ; vos missions seront les suivantes : Participer aux travaux BUILD/RUN sur le socle H2S : Gestion de l’obsolescence des composants, couche OS (TalosOS), composants Kube (Secret reloader, Cilium, ….) et applicatifs (ArgoCD, Prometheus, Graylog, ….) jobs.title: Ingénieur Support Cloud Infrastructures F/H
jobs.description: Et si les systèmes informatiques complexes et critiques de nos clients étaient votre nouveau terrain de jeu ? Nos clients sont des clients basés en France (banques et assurances, secteur public, domaine de la santé…). Ils attendent de nous un fonctionnement de service optimal pour leurs applications critiques : appels d’urgence, dossier patient numérique, paiements sécurisés entre autres. Votre rôle : déployer et assurer l’exploitation de ces systèmes, sous la responsabilité du responsable d'exploitation. Mener à bien le déploiement des nouveaux systèmes (BUILD) en relation étroite avec les chefs de projets client et nos architectes. Travailler avec les technologies d’IA, de BigData et de Machine Learning afin de rendre le service toujours plus pro-actif. Intégrer une posture DevOPs afin d’automatiser les tâches d’exploitation courantes ou critiques. Rejoignez notre équipe de déploiement et d’exploitation. Cette équipe, responsable de la « Gestion des Incidents » et de la « Gestion du Changement » sur ces infrastructures est basée en France. Elle travaille en étroite collaboration avec les équipes techniques de nos clients et avec les autres équipes d’Orange Cloud for Business, situées dans nos Majors Services Centers, en Inde, Égypte, Ile Maurice

Votre contrat pourra être d'une durée de 12, 24 ou 36 mois. Nous travaillons dans le souci de la performance pour mener à bien nos projets et satisfaire nos clients, dans un esprit d’entraide et dans la bonne humeur ! Nos projets sont de taille variable, très variés fonctionnellement et apportent de beaux défis techniques sur des technologies récentes.

jobs.title: National Account Manager
jobs.description: Apetito provides meals to over 20,000 children in 200+ education settings, and we’re entering an exciting new phase of growth. To support this, we’re recruiting a National Account Manager on a maternity cover contract (with potential to become permanent). This senior role will focus on securing large-scale national accounts across the UK, leading major tender submissions and driving new business outside of tenders. Working closely with the General Manager and a team of ten Regional Key Account Managers, you’ll help shape our sales strategy and contribute to our goal of reaching £25m+ annual turnover. We’re looking for a commercially astute, ambitious professional with experience winning large, complex accounts. You’ll be confident engaging stakeholders at all levels, skilled in negotiation and relationship-building, and ideally experienced in education, catering, or contract services. This is a national role involving regular travel and occasional overnight stays. It’s a rare opportunity to make a significant impact and help Revolutionise School Catering. Fixed time contract for 9 months.

jobs.title: Customer Service Advisor
jobs.description: This position is working 15 hours per week, any 2 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our customers are predominantly elderly and rely upon our office teams to take their orders over the phone or via the internet. The defining characteristics of our Customer Services Advisors are that they’re organised and efficient. However, apart from the technical skills that are required with the role, the most important attributes are that we demonstrate empathy, patience, kindness, friendliness, care and warmth to all our customers. The role involves the taking of customer orders and also the making of outbound courtesy calls. These calls are particularly important as many customers live alone and the phone calls they receive are often the only contact they may have in a single day. As such, many customers become very attached to their “friends” at Wiltshire Farm Foods. Wiltshire Farm Foods supplies frozen meal solutions direct to the consumer. We have a cold store to accommodate the stock necessary to be able to be able to deliver to our customers each week. The ultimate goal is to provide a service to our customers that exceed expectations while at the same time continuously improving efficiencies. 2 days a week, 15 hours £13.45 per hour + Benefits + Bonus

jobs.title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
jobs.description: This position is working 24 hours per week, any 3 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. View our latest TV ad here: https://www.wiltshirefarmfoods.com/blog/our-new-tv-ad-launches £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

jobs.title: Ingénieur(e) DevSecOps F/H
jobs.description: Description de l'entité Vous rejoignez l’équipe AOI, un groupe dynamique et engagé au sein de la Business Line Defense Center d’Orange. Cette équipe joue un rôle clé dans la sécurisation et l’innovation des solutions technologiques dédiées à la défense, en alliant expertise technique et esprit collaboratif pour relever les défis de demain. Description des missions DevSecOps : quand la sécurité rencontre l’automatisation, ça donne votre prochain défi ! Vos missions quotidiennes Vous prenez en charge la finalisation, le développement et la maintenance de la solution Qevlar AI, un outil d’investigation automatique des incidents de sécurité basé sur l’intelligence artificielle, déployé dans un environnement GCP et Kubernetes. Vous assurez la conception, le déploiement et la maintenance des pipelines CI/CD sécurisés pour garantir la qualité et la sécurité des livrables. Vous collaborez étroitement avec les équipes de développement et de sécurité pour intégrer les meilleures pratiques DevSecOps. Vous automatisez les processus de sécurité et de conformité tout au long du cycle de vie des applications. Vous surveillez et analysez les incidents de sécurité pour proposer des améliorations continues. THIS POSITION IS AN ONSITE DAILY ROLE. We are looking for a Senior Biostatistician for either our Maple Grove, MN, Santa Clara, CA or Burlington, MA locations. The Opportunity We are seeking an experienced, high caliber Senior Biostatistician to join Abbott’s medical device clinical research organization. This position includes providing statistical expertise in clinical study design, writing statistical analysis plans and reports, performing statistical analysis and programming, checking data quality, preparing analysis datasets, generating and/or validating data listing, and tables/reports. This position will also assist on additional study projects or tasks which may arise. Working under limited supervision by the statistical manager, provides statistical support to clinical study teams and external teams. This includes: statistical input into clinical study designs, endpoints, hypotheses tests and sample size calculations performing statistical analysis of clinical study data validating statistical a nalyses co nducted by statistical peers or colleagues. This position may also perform statistical analysis or provide statistical input on other projects as assigned by the statistical manager. Able to provide significant input into complex clinical study designs What You’ll Work On Job Duties: Provide statistical input into clinical study design, endpoints, hypotheses tests and sample size calculations Write statistical sections of protocols Write statistical analysis plans Provide input into data collection forms (Case Report Forms) and data management plan Provide input on study processes, especially those affecting scientific integrity and data quality Write or provide input into Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) and/or Clinical Events Committee (CEC) charter Ensure scientific integrity and data quality of clinical trials are preserved Participate in DMC meetings as appropriate Prepare statistical reports or statistical sections of clinical study reports Interpret statistical results Generate line listings for regulatory reporting as appropriate Perform validation of statistical analyses conducted by statistical peers or colleagues Ensures accuracy of report text and consistency between summary tables in the body of reports and the corresponding statistical tables and listings. Responsible for statistical methods Identifies and corrects common flaws in interpretation of results, inconsistency in presentation or inference, and adherence to Abbott report guidelines. Provides meaningful input to the development of a report strategy. Works collaboratively with peers to develop quality protocols, CRF, schema, and reports per agreed timelines. Provides accurate and timely answers to routine questions from clients, scientific insight when answering nonstandard questions, and independently pursues analyses suggested by the data. Communicates an understanding of basic clinical principles for his/her project and acts in accordance with those principles. Can clearly communicate basic statistical concepts to other scientists and nonscientists. Informs supervisor or manager on important issues in a timely manner . Required Qualifications: Masters in statistics or biostatistics with a minimum of 5 years of experience or Ph.D. in statistics or biostatistics with 3 years of experience. Able to write/describe statistical models of moderate complexity. Extensive experience with SAS is required ; experience with R, Winbugs , JMP, NCSS PASS and other statistical software are a plus. Knowledge of Bayesian and/or adaptive design methods and data mining are a plus. Bachelors Degree (± 16 years), BS/BA with 5+ years related work experience OR an equivalent combination of education and work experience, Masters Degree (± 18 years), MS with 3+ years of related work experience OR an equivalent combination of education and work experience Preferred Qualifications: Able to write/describe statistical models of moderate complexity. Knowledge of Bayesian and/or adaptive design methods and data mining are a plus. Minimum of 5 years of experience in medical device Clinical Research role is preferred. Adventist HealthCare was the first and is the largest healthcare provider in Montgomery County. If you want to make a difference in someone’s life every day, consider a position with a team of professionals who are doing just that, making a difference. Join the Adventist HealthCare team today, apply now to be considered! COVID-19 Vaccination Adventist HealthCare strongly recommends all applicants to be fully vaccinated for COVID-19 before commencing employment. Applicants may be required to furnish proof of vaccination. Tobacco and Drug Statement Tobacco use is a well-recognized preventable cause of death in the United States and an important public health issue. In order to promote and maintain a healthy work environment, Adventist HealthCare will not hire applicants for employment who either state that they are nicotine users or who test positive for nicotine and drug use. While some jurisdictions, including Maryland, permit the use of marijuana for medical purposes, marijuana continues to be classified as an illegal drug under the federal Controlled Substances Act.  As a result, medical marijuana use will not be accepted as a valid explanation for a positive drug test result. Adventist HealthCare will withdraw offers of employment to applicants who test positive for Cotinine (nicotine) and marijuana. Those testing positive are given the opportunity to re-apply in 90 days, if they can truthfully attest that they have not used any nicotine products in the past ninety (90) days and successfully pass follow-up testing. ("Nicotine products" include, but are not limited to: cigarettes, cigars, pipes, chewing tobacco, e-cigarettes, vaping products, hookah, and nicotine replacement products (e.g., nicotine gum, nicotine patches, nicotine lozenges, etc.). Equal Employment Opportunity Adventist HealthCare is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We are committed to attracting, engaging, and developing the best people to cultivate our mission-centric culture. Our goal is to have a welcoming, equitable, and safe place to work and grow for all employees, no matter their background. AHC does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, ethnicity, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy and related medical conditions, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Adventist HealthCare will make reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities, in accordance with applicable law.  Adventist HealthCare is a religious organization as defined under applicable law; however, it will endeavor to provide reasonable accommodations for applicants’ religious beliefs. We are seeking a dynamic and motivated individual to join our Warehouse and Transport team in an exciting rotational programme designed for growth and development into a management role. This is a fantastic opportunity for someone eager to learn and progress in a fast-paced, hands-on environment. As part of this role, you will rotate between various departments and key functions, gaining exposure to all aspects of warehouse operations, transport logistics, and customer service. Flexibility, adaptability, and a passion for leadership are essential as you grow with us and develop the skills necessary to step into a managerial role. We will create you a plan which will rotate you between customer service team, stock team, planning, driver +  Fleet Management and Warehousing. We will also be looking for you to focus on some specific projects as you complete these rotations. You will be reporting into the Warehouse manager you will work alongside all the senior management for the division. The operation is based at a National Distribution Centre, this incorporates a Frozen Storage and Order Fulfilment centre that operates 24 hours a day from Sunday Afternoon to Friday Night.  The core hours of operation for this role will be between 06:00 to 22:00 (Monday to Friday) in 8 hour shifts, with occasional requirement to work at night. This role will provide a graduate with: - Experience in a FMCG temperature controlled distribution operation - Exposure to the senior management team. - Opportunity to interact with and experience all parts of a business - Involvement with specific projects that they will be able to co-ordinate and play an active part in - The opportunity to develop general business skills and relationships

This is more than a process role—it’s about building a culture of relentless improvement at the heart of one of the UK’s most vital frozen supply chains. You will need a high degree of autonomy and judgement, and be trusted to lead the delivery of high-profile PR activity under the direction of the PR Manager. Sitting within the central Brand & Communications team, you will work closely with colleagues across the business and external partners to bring our stories to life, ensuring our voice is credible, consistent and values-led. Most importantly, you will help share how apetito and Wiltshire Farm Foods are making a real difference to customers, communities and colleagues every single day. jobs.title: NP- Adult Home Visit Program - F/T Orange County - Remote
jobs.description: Summary: MemorialCare Medical Group is seeking to add an outstanding Nurse Practitioner (NP) to its expanding Adult Home Visit Program in South Orange County. The program provides medical and palliative care to patients experiencing complex health challenges who are currently unable to visit their primary care physician (PCP). The program's goal is to provide timely, focused care that improves patient health and quality of life. This home visit program is coordinated on behalf of the patient’s PCP in the home, board-and-care, or assisted living setting. The NP will be part of an amazing interdisciplinary team and will receive assistance and work closely with other programs/departments. MemorialCare Medical Group is a physician-based organization with more than 2,000 employed and affiliated physicians in Orange and Los Angeles counties. MemorialCare Health System is listed among the top 20 percent of health systems nationwide by Thomson Reuters, one of the top 100 integrated healthcare networks nationwide, and the top 10 in the west region by IMS. MemorialCare Health System is the predominant fully integrated health system in the Southern Los Angeles County and Orange County with over 200 locations. Essential Functions and Responsibilities of the Job In this full-time role, you’ll be working independently to manage and coordinate medically complex patients with regards to the following: Provide history and physical as appropriate for each patient Effectively identify, evaluate, and address acute and chronic health conditions as well as disease prevention and health promotion issues Ability to perform many procedures, including wound closure, I&D, foreign body removal, etc. In the near future, may include providing palliative care in rotating clinic locations 10-20% of the time. Maintains legible, accurate and confidential medical records. Documents all medical evaluations, diagnoses, procedures, treatments, outcomes, education, referrals and consultation Coordinate and direct appropriate care for patient by means of an initial diagnosis and treatment, obtaining second opinions as necessary and consultation with contracted specialists Follow-up on referrals made to specialists to assess the results of the care, medication regimen and special treatment and ensure continuous care Establish procedures to contact patients when they miss appointments, require re-scheduling for additional visits, or confirming referrals to a specialist for care Coordinate patient referrals for other service Submit services with the usual and customary billed charges in a timely fashion, knowledgeable about HCC coding + Responsible for a mental health assessment, ruling out mental disorders due to a general medical condition, ruling out substance related disorders and identifying general medical conditions that cause or exacerbate psychological symptoms Establish and maintain effective relationships with members and their caregivers in collaboration with clinical staff, PCP’s, specialists, Social Workers, Case Managers, etc. Identify the appropriate level of care, i.e., hospitalization, home health, palliative care or hospice. Participate in clinical and operational data collection to demonstrate the impact of the program. Communicate with patients, families, and interdisciplinary teams around goals of care and advanced care planning. Experience/Qualifications/Certifications: 2-3 years NP experience in acute care, primary care, prior home health experience + Master’s Degree in Nursing (required) Active RN license in the State of California (required) Current BLS certification (required) Current Nurse Practitioner certification in the State of California (required) Current DEA license Bilingual in Spanish+ Financial & Benefits Mileage reimbursement Full and Comprehensive Benefits for Provider and Family Benefits to include: Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, Disability, Malpractice Insurance and Tail coverage 401k retirement plan with employer contribution 529 College Savings plan Generous PTO plan Base salary range: $131,000-$160,000+* *The salary range above is an estimated annual base salary for a full-time position and depending on experience. Our compensation model utilizes fair market value data specific to your specialty.

jobs.description: Ready to advance your marketing career - join HGA's Marketing team! Locations: Minneapolis, MN; Milwaukee, WI; Boston MA; Washington, DC; Santa Monica, San Francisco, or Sacramento, CA As a nationally recognized architecture, engineering, and planning firm, we are seeking a Senior Marketing Coordinator to support our marketing efforts within the Healthcare sector . If you're a creative, results-driven marketer who thrives in a fast-paced, collaborative environment, we want to hear from you! This individual will own the end-to-end process of producing marketing collateral, coordinating field marketing, and developing HGA stories to inspire action. From brainstorming ideas and writing content to coordinating / producing proposals and award submittals, we’re looking for someone who can help us target and win more specific clients and projects. In this role you will: Lead the end-to-end process – project management, content development, and document production – for marketing collateral for client prepositioning and pursuits, specifically: RFQ/P Responses including qualifications packages, proposals, and interviews that integrate creative storytelling and visual delivery. Collaborate with Healthcare practice group leaders, principals, and business developers to implement messaging strategy, define narrative approach, and write/proof copy for marketing content. Develop high-quality marketing content and collateral to support other marketing initiatives including project awards submissions, conferences, social media, speaking engagements, and publication advertising. Coordinate external (events, conferences, photoshoots) marketing activities and produce corresponding marketing collateral. Work in a highly interactive and cross-functional position, collaborating and cultivating positive relationships with multiple team members across various teams and offices. Partner with the Marketing and Communications team on the execution of strategic initiatives and champion marketing projects across teams as appropriate. Take initiative with a solution-focused mindset with an ability to work in ambiguity and pivot through the unexpected. You will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Journalism, Art History (or other related field) or a relevant combination of experience, education, and training 8+ years of professional experience writing marketing content, creating marketing collateral in Adobe InDesign, and managing multiple marketing projects from beginning to end Experience managing and producing RFQ/P responses in the architecture, engineering, and construction (AEC) industry preferred, ideally with a Healthcare focus Exceptional aptitude in Adobe InDesign and proficiency with other Adobe Creative Suite programs (Photoshop, Illustrator) Proficiency in Adobe Workfront, Canva, and/or VantagePoint is a plus Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Teams) Proofreading, editing and standardizing proposal text for proper grammar, spelling, comprehension and style while adhering to brand standardizations High attention to detail is critical Ability to work in a team and independently, self-starter and able to multitask workload to meet deadlines Mentorship experience Knowledge of Healthcare industry; building types, common healthcare verbiage, etc. Salary information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted . Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $89,300 - $106,000 (Mid-West) or $97,100 - $126,200 (East and West Coast) depending on qualifications and experience. Additional information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We design with impact by diving deep into the people and passions that shape every place. Empathy fuels us, curiosity drives us, and meaningful work leads to innovation. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Join our nationally recognized, award-winning team of architects and engineers—and help us shape what’s next. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume for consideration.

jobs.description: Thoracic Surgeon, MemorialCare Medical Group Primary Location: MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center – Long Beach, CA Additional Practice Sites: Orange Coast Medical Center & Saddleback Medical Center MemorialCare Heart and Vascular Institute is seeking a Board Certified or Board Eligible, robotic-trained, Thoracic Surgeon to join our well-established, physician-led medical group in Southern California. The primary clinical practice will be based at MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center, with opportunities to perform thoracic surgical cases at Orange Coast Medical Center and Saddleback Medical Center as part of our coordinated, multi-campus care model. Position Highlights Employed physician position with MemorialCare Medical Group Integrated care environment with access to advanced surgical technology and multidisciplinary teams Opportunity to serve a diverse patient population across multiple MemorialCare campuses Collegial, quality-focused culture with strong clinical and operational support Position Responsibilities The Thoracic Surgeon will provide comprehensive inpatient and outpatient surgical care for patients with diseases and disorders of the thoracic cavity. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to: Evaluate, diagnose, and manage conditions affecting the lungs, pleura, mediastinum, esophagus, diaphragm, and chest wall Perform a full range of general thoracic surgical procedures, including both open and minimally invasive techniques (VATS and/or robotic; 60-70% of our volume is robotic), such as lung resections, lobectomies, mediastinal mass resections, and esophageal procedures Conduct thorough pre‑operative assessments, including review of diagnostic imaging and test results (e.g., CT scans, PET scans, pulmonary function tests), and formulate individualized surgical treatment plans Provide postoperative management and follow‑up care, including monitoring recovery, managing complications, and coordinating ongoing care across the continuum Collaborate closely with multidisciplinary teams, including pulmonology, oncology, gastroenterology, radiology, anesthesia, critical care, and nursing, to ensure high‑quality, coordinated patient care Participate in inpatient consults, surgical call coverage, and rounding responsibilities at MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center, with additional surgical coverage at Orange Coast Medical Center and Saddleback Medical Center as clinically indicated Educate patients and their families regarding diagnoses, surgical options, procedural risks and benefits, and postoperative recovery expectations in a clear and compassionate manner Maintain accurate, timely, and complete medical documentation in compliance with MemorialCare policies, regulatory requirements, and quality standards Support quality improvement initiatives, peer review activities, and evidence‑based practice guidelines to enhance patient outcomes and safety Stay current with advancements in thoracic surgery techniques, technology, and best practices through continuing medical education and professional development Qualifications Board Certified or Board Eligible in Thoracic Surgery Active California medical license (or ability to obtain) Eligible to work in the United States Commitment to clinical excellence, patient-centered care, and collaboration within a multidisciplinary team Compensation & Benefits Competitive compensation package Shareholder / Partner eligibility after two years of employment Comprehensive benefits package for Physician and family Benefits include: Medical, dental, vision, life Insurance, disability, 401(k) retirement plan with employer matching, 529 College Savings plan, generous PTO plan Relocation assistance Professional liability insurance About MemorialCare Medical Group MemorialCare Medical Group is a physician-led, not-for-profit medical group aligned with MemorialCare Medical Foundation within MemorialCare Health System. We are dedicated to delivering high-quality, evidence-based care while fostering physician engagement, professional growth, and work-life balance. About Long Beach, California Located in coastal Southern California, Long Beach offers the rare combination of coastal Southern California living with true accessibility and livability. The hospital is minutes from the ocean, with year-round temperate weather and easy access to surfing, cycling, and waterfront living. Within a short drive, you can be in Manhattan Beach, Newport Beach, or Laguna Beach—each offering iconic beaches, surf culture, and coastal neighborhoods. The region is home to some of the best dining in Southern California, from waterfront seafood to Michelin-recognized restaurants, along with vibrant arts, boating, and outdoor recreation. You can practice at an elite level while living steps from the Pacific—without sacrificing commute time or family life.

jobs.description: JOB DESCRIPTION: About Abbott Abbott is a global healthcare leader, creating breakthrough science to improve people’s health. We’re always looking towards the future, anticipating changes in medical science and technology. Working at Abbott At Abbott, you can do work that matters, grow, and learn, care for yourself and family, be your true self and live a full life. You will have access to: Career development with an international company where you can grow the career you dream of . A company recognized as a great place to work in dozens of countries around the world and named one of the most admired companies in the world by Fortune. A company that is recognized as one of the best big companies to work for as well as a best place to work for diversity, working mothers, female executives, and scientists. The Opportunity This position works out of our Cairo Office in the Established Pharmaceutical Division where we are committed to bringing the benefits of our trusted medicines to more people in the world’s fastest-growing countries. Our broad portfolio of high-quality and differentiated branded generic medicines reaches across multiple therapeutic areas including gastroenterology, women's health, cardiometabolic, pain management/central nervous system, and respiratory. As a Product Manager, you’ll have the chance to lead a group of initiatives and campaign, driven by insights and based on strategic planning and financial acumen. You’ll manage the brand equity, collaborate cross-fucntionally and influence decision-makers for innovative initiatives to grow the brands and manage their life cycle. What You’ll Do: Develops annual TA strategies and brand plans in coordination with the country’s relevant cross-functional stakeholders. Develops annual & quarterly tactical plan of activities for relevant brands, in coordination with Sales & Marketing stakeholders. Align with regional & global marketing teams on brands strategies & updates, while insuring adherence to global brands strategy implementation. Plan and execute HCPs’ engagement educational programs & activities to raise the awareness about brands & relevant therapeutic areas. Train sales force on product knowledge and brand strategies. Generate and Analyze market insight & researches to optimize the decision-making process. Relevant KOL management in coordination with relevant commercial partners. Integral member of the demand and supply review, and active participation in forecasting and market projections. Regularly track the execution tactical plans and the impact of activities on overall performance, optimize ROI. Manage contacts and ensure strategic alliance between Abbott functional teams (Finance, Demand & Logistics management, Distribution, Sales & Marketing team), through building a strong internal network and providing business rationale, reach consensus, resolve conflicts, escalate issues. Regularly collect and provide Management with information about marketing & commercial activities of other pharmaceutical companies and identify market trends. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in pharmaceutical sciences. Minimum 6 years of experience in a commercial role in the pharma industry. Minimum 2 years of experience in a marketing role in the pharma industry. Excellent communication skills with high proficiency in spoken and written English & Arabic. Proficient MS office tools (PowerPoint & Excel), and fair understanding of digital promotion tools. Remarkable KOLs’ engagement record. Thinks strategically and demonstrates learning agility, with a growth mindset. Good financial and business acumen. Preferred Qualifications People management experience. MBA or a marketing post-graduate degree. Proven ability to influence cross-functional teams, internal and external stakeholders. Apply Now Follow your career aspirations to Abbott for diverse opportunities with a company that can help you build your future and live your best life. Abbott is an Equal Opportunity Employer, committed to employee diversity. Connect with us at www.abbott.com , on Facebook at www.facebook.com/Abbott and on Twitter @AbbottNews and @AbbottGlobal. jobs.title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
jobs.description: This position is working 24 hours per week, any 3 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

jobs.description: Lead Innovative Electrical Design at HGA - Washington, DC or Boston, MA! Bring your passion and expertise in government, commercial, corporate, higher education, and/or performing arts projects to HGA , an award-winning, integrated architecture, engineering, and planning firm. As a Senior Electrical Project Engineer , you will play a pivotal role in leading and managing large-scale electrical projects and teams—locally and nationally. We’re looking for a highly organized, communicative, and effective team player with a passion for high-quality design and innovative problem-solving . If you thrive on leading teams, ensuring technical excellence, and delivering impactful designs, this is an exciting opportunity to grow your career. What You’ll Do: Lead electrical design on large-scale projects across various sectors, including corporate , higher education , and performing arts. Manage project teams locally and nationally to ensure high-quality, timely delivery of electrical systems and solutions. Develop and review electrical designs, including power distribution, lighting systems, and control systems, ensuring compliance with codes and client requirements. Provide technical leadership and mentorship to junior engineers and design professionals. Collaborate with architects, engineers, and other stakeholders to integrate electrical systems into innovative, functional designs. Communicate effectively with clients, consultants, and project teams to drive projects forward and ensure expectations are met. In this role you will: Assess the requirements of a project, break a project into tasks, and work with the design team to determine scope, budget, and staffing. Write scopes of work for electrical design based on preliminary review and meetings with the client. Prepare engineering studies and schematic design for new systems and analyze operation of existing systems. Present complex engineering analysis clearly and effectively to owner and design teams. Document existing conditions and perform complex and detailed troubleshooting. Resolve a variety of complex problems, i.e. conflicting design requirements, code issues, varying design options, and difficult condition requirements. Research non-routine code issues and obtain interpretations from code officials. Produce sketches and system schematics for complicated design tasks. Prepare engineering opinion of probable cost for electrical systems and review contractor cost estimates. Develop calculations using engineering formulas and skills for Electrical designs such as whole building electrical loads, lighting calculations, power distribution calculations, equipment sizing, editing specification sections, and creating punch lists. Review drawings and documents to assure compliance with company design and production standards. Assure that drawings and designs are in accordance with design standards. Develop and maintain a thorough understanding of construction processes and delivery methods. Assist in the education and development of design staff and act as a resource for questions. You will need to have: BS or MS degree in electrical engineering. Professional licensure is required. 12+ years of experience in leading design of government, commercial, corporate, higher education, and/or performing arts project. Documented experience with an MEP engineering firm or A/E firm providing electrical design. Expertise in National Fire Protection Association Codes (NFPA) and an understanding of applicable (city, state, federal) requirements and pertinent standards is essential. Active government security clearance, or eligibility to obtain and maintain the required clearance for project assignments. Working knowledge of Revit software. Familiarity with electrical analysis software (SKM, Easy Power) and knowledge of the current sustainable technologies applicable to the design of commercial electrical systems. Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary range:  $121.000 - $147,800 depending on experience and expertise. Additional Information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We design with impact by diving deep into the people and passions that shape every place. Empathy fuels us, curiosity drives us, and meaningful work leads to innovation. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Join our nationally recognized, award-winning team of architects, engineers, and interior designers — help us shape what’s next. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume for consideration.

jobs.description: Bring your design‑sensitive, technically ambitious expertise to HGA, where architecture, engineering, and innovation converge to shape environments that matter! You’ll have the opportunity to influence projects in healthcare , corporate headquarters , laboratories , museums , government facilities , higher education , and infrastructure —all while collaborating with some of the most forward‑thinking professionals in the industry. What You’ll Bring Deep expertise in building codes and industry standards Mastery of mechanical engineering principles and system design Strong analytical and creative problem‑solving skills Proven leadership and collaboration across diverse teams A passion for innovation, mentorship, and elevating design performance As building systems and technologies evolve, we’re looking for leaders who thrive in complexity and bring precision, curiosity, and purpose to every challenge. This is more than a role—it’s an opportunity to shape the built environment with a firm that values insight, collaboration, and technical excellence. This is more than a role—it’s a chance to shape the built environment with a firm that values insight, collaboration, and technical excellence. Ready to build what’s next? We’d love to hear from you. In this role you will: Be responsible for HVAC engineering design including calculating heating and cooling loads, select equipment, layout duct and piping, create and edit project details, and write and edit project engineering specifications. Be responsible for plumbing systems design including calculating capacities and loads, laying out piping systems and details, and editing project specifications. participate in design review meetings and workshops; Resolve a variety of complex design challenges including conflicting Owner and project requirements, application of different system types, or challenging field conditions. Research design options and document findings for the project team. Assure drawings and designs are in accordance with local codes and design standards. Coordinate with architectural and engineering disciplines as applicable. You will need to have: BS. degree in Mechanical Engineering or Architectural Engineering 12+ years of consulting engineering experience with emphasis on healthcare projects for multiple client types Professional Engineer registration recognized by a state licensing board Knowledge in building controls is desired but not required Revit, BIM, Trane/Trace, IES and Microsoft Office skills required Must be in Rochester or willing to relocate ( relocation assistance provided by HGA ) Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $87,100 - $119,800. Additional Information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We design with impact by diving deep into the people and passions that shape every place. Empathy fuels us, curiosity drives us, and meaningful work leads to innovation. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Join our nationally recognized, award-winning team of architects, engineers, and interior designers — help us shape what’s next. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume for consideration.

jobs.title: Quality & Food Safety Auditor
jobs.description: The technical department can be described as the ‘Conscience of the Business’ with a core remit of keeping the business informed to ensure strong strategic and tactical decision, provide customer and supplier confidence in relation to food safety and quality standards and to safeguard the business by maintaining a strong due diligence defense. This is achieved by providing assurance that legal, customer, supplier and business standards of food quality and safety are maintained, ensuring procedural compliance, understanding and effectiveness and driving remedial actions to resolve the root cause of any issues and prevent recurrence. Additionally, the department has a responsibility to drive continuous improvement as an outcome of the core remit by setting standards and keeping abreast of developments that may impact the food industry. This role is working 36 hours per week, Friday - Sunday, 6am-6pm.

jobs.title: EMEA HE&R Region Lead
jobs.description: JOB DESCRIPTION: The EMEA Health Economics & Reimbursement (HE&R) Lead is a senior regional executive responsible for ensuring high‑quality access execution across the EMEA region. This includes leading a team of 6+ country HE&R Leads to deliver reimbursement, coverage, pricing, and reimbursement policy outcomes that enable sustainable access and drive business growth for Abbott’s medical device portfolio. This role serves as a critical partner to EMEA Divisional Vice Presidents, actively engaging to understand their business priorities, elevating clear access considerations, proactively identifying, evaluating and educating on shifting market dynamics, and delivering aligned and integrated action plans through regional HE&R business leaders and country teams. As a member of the HE&R Leadership Team, this role ensures that EMEA market requirements are incorporated into global product development, evidence strategies, and launch planning. When evidence or policy information is incomplete, this leader provides structured, risk‑adjusted recommendations to enable enterprise decision‑making; reflecting global HE&R leadership expectations for lifecycle integration. The role also partners closely with Government Affairs to monitor reimbursement‑relevant policy shifts, evaluate business impact, and shape Abbott’s external advocacy strategy, maintaining a targeted presence in regional professional societies and forums aligned with the EMEA access agenda. ROLES & RESPONSIBILITIES Lead and supervise 6+ Country HE&R teams across EMEA, ensuring consistent, competitive, high‑quality execution of reimbursement and pricing strategies. Establish an operating rhythm for performance reviews, barrier escalation, best‑practice sharing, and launch readiness to ensure accountability and execution discipline. Ensure country-level plans are aligned to regional priorities and address local payer, HTA, and funding requirements. Proactively identify risks in access execution and drive cross-functional mitigation plans with clear owners and timelines Serve as a trusted access advisor to EMEA Divisional Vice Presidents, and partner to EMEA HE&R Regional Business Leaders, by translating business priorities into clear access considerations and in-market execution plans. Provide concise, actionable guidance on access trade-offs, portfolio prioritization, and resource needs. Support the development of access components of regional business plans with the regional HE&R business leads, ensuring alignment across Commercial, Medical, Clinical and Regulatory teams at the country level. Ensure country‑level pathway requirements are surfaced to Global/Regional Business Leads, and incorporated into local launch planning Ensure evidence requirements, payer expectations, and pathway prerequisites are systematically incorporated early into global and regional planning. When information is incomplete, lead structured risk assessments and propose risk-adjusted recommendations, including scenario assumptions and decision triggers. Partner with Government Affairs to track reimbursement, pricing, HTA, and funding policy dynamics across EMEA and translate developments into business impact assessments. Shape strategic responses and build business preparedness plans to navigate policy changes. Coordinate strategic functional representation in regional forums, societies, and coalitions aligned to advancing the EMEA access agenda Build, develop, and retain a high-performing team of country HE&R leaders and support their professional growth. Drive regional capability building in payer engagement, HTA navigation, economic value communication, and access-focused launch readiness. Provide clear performance expectations, coaching, succession planning, and accountability frameworks Oversee regional budget planning and resource allocation. Elevate access literacy across regional and country teams by providing structured guidance, tools, education and communication frameworks. Represent EMEA HE&R execution on the Global HE&R leadership team EDUCATION & COMPETENCIES Advanced degree in an associated field required (e.g., MPH, MBA, PhD, Health Economics, Public Policy, Health Services Research) 12+ years’ experience in Market Access, Reimbursement, Health Economics, Payer Strategy, or related roles in medical devices or healthcare; Previous experience in country level access leadership preferred Demonstrated success leading country access teams or driving access outcomes across multiple EMEA markets. Strong business partnership skills with the ability to influence senior leadership and shape strategy Proven ability to synthesize complex policy or evidence information into clear, actionable insights. Strong analytical, strategic thinking, and communication skills. Experience leading teams, developing talent, and fostering execution accountability. Ability to navigate complex matrix organizations and represent the function effectively in regional and global governance processes. SUCCESS METRICS (Internal) Execution of reimbursement and pricing strategy across priority EMEA markets. Achievement of competitive country-level access outcomes, including improved time-to-access metrics and reduced execution barriers. Regional business preparedness and ability to anticipate and respond to policy changes in collaboration with Government Affairs and key business partners. Quality of partnership with EMEA DVPs and cross-functional leaders, as reflected in alignment, execution discipline, and strategic influence. Integration of EMEA requirements into global evidence and product planning, including risk-adjusted recommendations when required. Team performance and capability growth, including retention and development of country HE&R leaders. Impact of external advocacy engagements aligned to the EMEA access agenda, reinforcing expectations from prior regional and global HE&R roles The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted.
