jobs.title: Productie Planner
jobs.description: JOB DESCRIPTION: Over Abbott Abbott is een wereldwijde leider in de gezondheidszorg en zet zich in om mensen te helpen langer en beter te leven. Met ongeveer 130.000 medewerkers in meer dan 160 landen ontwikkelt Abbott een breed portfolio op het gebied van diagnostiek, medische hulpmiddelen, voeding en geneesmiddelen. In Nederland werken dagelijks circa 2.000 collega’s op 9 locaties aan duurzame oplossingen voor enkele van de belangrijkste uitdagingen binnen de gezondheidszorg van vandaag. Over Abbott Nutrition Abbott Nutrition levert wetenschappelijk onderbouwde voedingsproducten voor mensen van alle leeftijden. Onze producten ondersteunen de groei van kinderen en helpen te voorzien in de unieke voedingsbehoeften van mensen met chronische aandoeningen. Op onze productielocatie in Zwolle produceren wij verschillende vertrouwde voedingsmerken voor kinderen en volwassenen, waaronder PediaSure, Isomil en Ensure. Bij Abbott ontwikkelen we technologieën die levens veranderen en worden we gedreven door onze missie om voor iedereen, in elke levensfase, de juiste zorg te bieden. Wil jij deel uitmaken van deze missie en een bijdrage leveren aan de maatschappij? Ter versterking van onze productielocatie zijn wij op zoek naar een gemotiveerde en analytische Productieplanner die graag werkt op het snijvlak van productie, logistiek en service excellence. Dit betreft een kantoorfunctie in Zwolle. Wat ga je doen Als Productieplanner speel je een sleutelrol in het waarborgen van een betrouwbare supply chain en operationele continuïteit. Je werkt nauw samen met de lokale productieteams en het centrale planningsteam in Zwolle om alles soepel te laten verlopen. Je verantwoordelijkheden zijn onder andere: Monitoren en verbeteren van interne en externe servicelevels Dagelijkse afstemming met interne en externe stakeholders over prioriteiten binnen productie-, voorraad- en transportplanning Optimaliseren van analyses en KPI‑rapportages (bijvoorbeeld in Power BI) Fungeren als back‑up binnen het planningsteam om continuïteit te waarborgen Snel reageren op ad‑hoc verzoeken binnen de supply chain Wat breng je mee Een afgeronde hbo‑opleiding (Logistiek, Supply Chain Management, Bedrijfskunde of vergelijkbaar) Sterke analytische vaardigheden en een proactieve werkhouding Ervaring in productieplanning of supply chain is een sterke pré Goede communicatieve vaardigheden en het vermogen om cross‑functioneel samen te werken Flexibiliteit en veerkracht in een dynamische productieomgeving Uitstekende Excel‑vaardigheden; ervaring met Power BI is een plus Vloeiend in Nederlands en Engels (mondeling en schriftelijk) Beschikbaar voor een fulltime dienstverband (40 uur per week) Wat wij bieden Een uitdagende functie binnen een wereldwijde organisatie met een betekenisvolle missie Een professionele en samenwerkingsgerichte werkomgeving in Zwolle Ruimte voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling, opleidingen en doorgroeimogelijkheden Een marktconform salaris en aantrekkelijke arbeidsvoorwaarden Een moderne werkomgeving waar initiatief en verbeterideeën worden gewaardeerd Solliciteren Ben je enthousiast over deze functie? Dien dan je sollicitatie in via de knop ‘Apply’ hierboven. Sollicitaties worden doorlopend beoordeeld. The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. jobs.description: En tant que Service Delivery Manager, vous êtes l’interlocuteur privilégié du client dans le cadre d'un contrat de services. A ce titre, vous êtes le garant du respect des engagements, de la Qualité de Service globale et de la satisfaction client ainsi que de la rentabilité opérationnelle de ses comptes. Le périmètre fonctionnel des prestations attendues est décomposé en 4 lots : Lot 1 : BUILD Élaborer des solutions standard de cybersécurité et des services associés, sur la base des besoins exprimés par la Compagnie, à des coûts maîtrisés. Lot 2.1: DEPLOY Déployer des solutions et des services de cybersécurité sur nos sites industriels : installation/configuration, pilotage Lot 2.2: RUN Maintenir en conditions opérationnelles et de sécurité (MCO/MCS) les solutions et services de cybersécurité conformément aux SLA définis (Servie Level Agreement). Lot 3: Cyber Operations Assister les sites à mettre en oeuvre des mesures de remédiation lorsque des alertes et des incidents de cybersécurité sont signalés par le SIEM/SOC (Security Operation Centre) du client. Le Service Delivery Manager (SDM) assure l’interface entre le client et les différentes prestations des lots du dispositif, il est le point d’entrée qui peut solliciter tout interlocuteur interne pour répondre à la demande client. Vos missions : En relation direct avec les équipes d’exploitation, votre mission comprend les activités suivantes : Analyser les enjeux et criticités client au sein des équipes opérationnelles Piloter, dans certains cas, la mise en place du service managé (outils d’exploitation, supervision) Elaborer le reporting et assurer l’animation des comités Gérer et anticiper les situations de crise Garantir le respect des SLA (Service Level Agreement) auprès des clients Piloter les plans d’actions mis en place avec les équipes clients et les prestataires externes Suivre la satisfaction client, proposer un plan d’amélioration continu et optimiser les services contractualisés Assurer un rôle de conseil et participer au développement de la relation commerciale en identifiant les opportunités business et en les adressant aux équipes commerciales. Rendre compte de son activité et alerter si nécessaire La langue de communication orale pour la gouvernance du contrat sera le français. L’anglais sera utilisé dans les correspondances avec les sites, hormis pour les sites francophones. Tous les documents et livrables écrits seront rédigés en anglais.

jobs.title: Technical Compliance  Manager
jobs.description: At apetito, we are committed to delivering the highest standards of food safety, quality and compliance. We’re looking for an experienced Technical Compliance Manager to help us maintain and continually improve these standards. As Technical Compliance Manager, you will lead the validation, verification and audit of our UK food safety and quality systems. You’ll work closely with technical and operational teams to ensure full compliance with legal requirements and maintain our AA grade BRCGS Food Safety certification. This is a fantastic time to join us. As we grow towards £300m in turnover, we’re investing in a new, state‑of‑the‑art frozen food manufacturing facility alongside our existing site in Trowbridge. Your responsibilities will cover both sites, offering significant opportunity for development as our operations expand.

Ces processus doivent répondre aux objectifs d’excellence d’OBS SA aussi bien au niveau de l’expérience salarié qu’à celui de la performance opérationnelle des acteurs RH. Elle est le garant du bon fonctionnement des systèmes d’informations RH (le SIRH) mais aussi est force de proposition pour l’adaptation des outils, en adéquation avec les évolutions des besoins des utilisateurs. L’application permet une gestion fluide et intégrée de la paie et du déclaratif. Nous sommes à la recherche de notre futur(e) AMOA SIRH – Paie / GA / DSN. Vous interviendrez sur les applications liées à la paie , à la gestion administrative et à la DSN principalement sur la partie RUN. Votre rôle sera, en appui au responsable SIRH Paie d’assurer l’expertise, la conformité réglementaire du RUN et de participer éventuellement à des projets annexes impactant l’univers SIRH Paie. Vous serez accompagné sur votre périmètre par le responsable SIRH en binôme et vous serez en soutien auprès des équipes métier (équipes de paie, administration du personnel) dont l’objectif primordial est la satisfaction de nos clients (salariés) via l’excellence de la gestion de la paie et de leur expérience utilisateur. Delivery Execution & Monitoring • Monitor daily B2B2C order fulfillment from CLPs to Shipping Service Provider of all platforms. • Track delivery performance against SLA, lead time, Shop Health of fulfillment, and service KPIs across O2O operations. • Ensure timely handover of orders from CLPs to shipping providers. • Follow up the return package handed over to CLPs on time. • Follow up on daily fulfillment execution, identifying risks that may impact delivery performance 2. Logistics Shipping Provider Coordination • Coordinate with delivery service providers (e.g. Viettel Post, J&T Express,…) to ensure on time pickups and deliveries. • Align pickup schedules and volume forecasts with logistics partners to support smooth operations. • Follow up with shipping providers to ensure platform SLA compliance. • Escalate and resolve delivery delays, exceptions, or failed deliveries. 3. Internal & External Coordination Work closely with O2O, 3A Logistics, CLPs, Sales & Trade, Platforms to: • Ensure order readiness and inventory availability • Confirm pickup timing • Address operational gaps impacting fulfillment and delivery • Provide daily operational support to CLPs to ensure orders are processed and delivered on time. • Coordinate with internal functions to ensure store onboarding is completed on time with full operational awareness. • Train CLPs on standard packaging and fulfillment requirements for each platform to ensure consistent execution and compliance. 4. Performance Tracking, Inventory & Reporting • Consolidate and prepare daily / weekly supply chain and delivery reports (SLA, backlog, exceptions). • Monitor and follow up on daily inventory status (products & gifts) at store level in line with demand planning. • Track order management and fulfillment performance, working with cross functional teams to address gaps. • Support performance reviews with internal stakeholders and logistics partners. • Coordinate the development and rollout of standard O2O fulfillment reports. 5. Issue Resolution & Continuous Improvement • Manage delivery related incidents end to end, including: Fast delivery rate, late delivery rate, cross-region rate, • Identify recurring fulfillment and delivery issues and propose corrective and preventive actions. • Support the rollout of new supply chain systems and tools, acting as a key user / SME for order management. • Support the Manager in reviewing, sharpening, and improving fulfillment SOPs to enhance operational efficiency and standardization. MINIMUM BACKGROUND/ EXPERIENCE REQUIRED: • Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Logistics, Business, or related fields • Project Management & Supply Chain Management • 1–3 years of experience in delivery operations, logistics coordination, or e commerce fulfillment • Experience with B2B2C / O2O / last mile delivery is an advantage • Strong coordination and follow up skills • Good data tracking and reporting capability • Ability to work with multiple stakeholders under time pressure • Problem solving and execution oriented mindset • Teamwork and cooperation • English skill in intermediate • Good skill on Power BI, Power Point & Excel. jobs.description: Vos missions : En mission interne, vous interviendrez au sein du SOC d'un de nos clients. L’objectif du service Security Operation Center est de lutter contre la cybercriminalité en mettant en œuvre les solutions techniques ou fonctionnelles nécessaires à la détection des tentatives d’intrusions sur le SI, le réseau, les plates-formes de services et les infrastructures. Le service a donc une vision transverse sur les menaces adressant les collaborateurs mais aussi les outils de production de ce client. La mission est orientée en différentes activités : Gestion des événements de sécurité (RUN) : en tant qu'analyste SOC, vous assurez la sécurité défensive de ce client au sein d’une équipe d’ analystes. Vous avez un rôle d'expertise sur le traitement des incidents de sécurité. Vous aurez en charge : La surveillance et le traitement des évènements et des alertes de sécurité ; L'analyse des données et l'investigation avancée des incidents ; La coordination avec l'ensemble des acteurs du client pour la résolution des incidents et l'accompagnement à la remédiation ; La participation au reporting des incidents de sécurité, de l'activité opérationnelle et la fourniture d'indicateurs ; Amélioration continue : gestion des changements et optimisations : En tant qu'analyste SOC, vous êtes acteur de l’amélioration continue. Vous aurez en charge en lien avec notre Security Manager : L'amélioration continue des systèmes de corrélation de la sécurité ; Les optimisations en matière de détection des menaces ; La veille technologique des menaces, des attaques et des vulnérabilités. Accompagnement de la croissance du périmètre et des évolutions technologiques du SOC : le périmètre du SOC est voué à croître suivant le planning d'intégration de notre client. En tant qu’analyste SOC, vous serez acteur de cette croissance en réalisant : L'intégration de nouveaux périmètres au sein de la détection du SOC La proposition et mise en œuvre de nouvelles règles de détection L'intégration de nouveaux outils au sein de l'activité du SOC Les technologies de détection utilisées par notre client évoluent : en tant qu’analyste SOC, vous êtes acteur de la migration technologique et de la continuité de service en vous formant à des nouveaux outils de manière régulière. Pour assurer la continuité de service de notre client, des astreintes sont à réaliser. Afin de vous accompagner dans cette mission, vous serez en lien direct avec les équipes du CyberSOC d'Orange Cyberdefense qui vous permettront de progresser sur les différents outils et méthodes de traitement des incidents de sécurité. Le poste nécessite de satisfaire aux obligations relatives à la Protection du Secret de la Défense Nationale. Ce poste peut être soumis, selon le besoin, à une enquête administrative.

jobs.description: Our customers are predominantly elderly and rely upon our office teams to take their orders over the phone or via the internet. T he ultimate goal is to provide a service to our customers that exceeds expectations while at the same time continuously improving efficiencies. Physician, Pediatric Endocrinology (full-time, outpatient and inpatient)
jobs.description: Physician, Pediatric Endocrinology MemorialCare Medical Group Practice Location Cherese Mari Laulhere Children’s Village Miller Children’s & Women’s Hospital Long Beach / MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center Campus Long Beach, California Position Summary MemorialCare Medical Group is seeking a BC/BE Pediatric Endocrinologist to provide high-quality, family-centered care in a community-based pediatric specialty practice. This position is based at the Cherese Mari Laulhere Children’s Village at Miller Children’s & Women’s Hospital Long Beach, located on the campus of MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center. The Pediatric Endocrinologist provides comprehensive specialty medical care to pediatric patients with endocrine and metabolic disorders in both ambulatory and inpatient settings. The role includes evaluation, diagnosis, treatment, and ongoing management of conditions within the scope of pediatric endocrinology, consistent with MemorialCare Medical Group clinical standards, policies, and applicable regulations. Essential Functions and Responsibilities Clinical Care Evaluate, diagnose, and manage pediatric patients with endocrine and metabolic conditions including diabetes mellitus, thyroid disease, growth and pubertal disorders, adrenal and pituitary diseases, disorders of sexual development, and calcium/bone metabolism disorders. Provide outpatient ambulatory care including initial consultations, longitudinal management, follow-up visits, and care coordination. Provide inpatient consultation services for hospitalized pediatric patients and participate in the assigned call schedule. Develop and implement individualized treatment plans in accordance with current standards of pediatric endocrine care. Order, interpret, and act upon laboratory, imaging, and diagnostic studies relevant to pediatric endocrinology. Prescribe medications, therapies, and medical devices (e.g., insulin pumps, continuous glucose monitoring) as clinically indicated. Document encounters accurately and timely in the electronic health record (EHR) in accordance with policy and regulatory requirements. Collaboration and Care Coordination Collaborate with neonatologists, pediatric intensivists, pediatric emergency medicine physicians, and other other physician colleagues, nurses, diabetes educators, dietitians, social workers, and other allied health professionals to ensure coordinated, family-centered care. Participate in interdisciplinary case discussions, clinical pathways, and care planning as appropriate. Communicate effectively with patients, families, and caregivers regarding diagnoses, treatment plans, and disease management. Quality, Compliance, and Professional Practice Adhere to MemorialCare Medical Group policies, clinical protocols, and quality standards. Comply with federal, state, and local regulations, including documentation, privacy, coding, and billing requirements. Participate in quality improvement, patient safety, and performance improvement initiatives as assigned. Maintain current knowledge of developments in pediatric endocrinology and engage in continuing medical education (CME). Participate in medical staff meetings, committees, and other administrative duties as required. Teaching and Program Support (as applicable) Participate in teaching and mentoring of medical students, residents, or fellows, if applicable. Support development of pediatric endocrine programs, pathways, and clinical initiatives. Qualifications Required MD or DO from an accredited medical school. Completion of an accredited Pediatric Residency Program. Completion of an accredited Pediatric Endocrinology Fellowship Program. Board Certification/Board Eligibility in Pediatric Endocrinology by the American Board of Pediatrics (ABP). Active, unrestricted California medical license (or eligibility to obtain prior to start). Current DEA registration. Eligibility for and maintenance of hospital medical staff privileges at MemorialCare-affiliated facilities. · Preferred Clinical experience in both inpatient and outpatient pediatric endocrinology settings. Experience with diabetes technologies, including insulin pumps and continuous glucose monitoring (CGM). Experience in multidisciplinary pediatric care and population health initiatives. Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities Comprehensive knowledge of pediatric endocrine diseases and standards of care. Ability to provide age-appropriate, family-centered care. Strong clinical judgment and diagnostic decision-making skills. Effective written and verbal communication skills. Ability to work collaboratively within a multidisciplinary healthcare team. Proficiency with EHR systems and clinical documentation. Commitment to professionalism, ethical practice, and continuous improvement. Work Environment Work is performed in clinical settings including outpatient clinics and hospital inpatient units. May involve exposure to communicable diseases, bodily fluids, chemicals, and other hazards typical of a healthcare environment. Requires adherence to infection prevention and safety protocols. Physical Requirements Ability to sit, stand, and walk for extended periods within clinical and hospital environments. Ability to perform physical examinations and clinical procedures typical of pediatric endocrine practice. Visual, auditory, and motor skills sufficient to provide safe and effective patient care. Ability to work extended hours, evenings, weekends, and call shifts as necessary. May include travel to outpatient specialty clinics such as Torrance and Fountain Valley. Schedule and Call This position includes scheduled ambulatory clinic sessions and inpatient consultation coverage. Participation in the division call schedule is required; specific rotation and frequency are determined by departmental needs. Supervisory Responsibility May provide clinical direction to residents, students, nursing staff, diabetic educators, and allied health staff as applicable. May serve as a supervising physician for the Group’s Advanced Health Practitioner(s) in Pediatric Endocrinology, as applicable. Compensation and Benefits Competitive compensation package with a base salary and performance-based compensation. Comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with matching, paid time off, and CME allowance.

jobs.title: Industrial Engineer
jobs.description: JOB DESCRIPTION: About Abbott Abbott is a global healthcare leader that helps people live more fully at all stages of life. Our portfolio of life-changing technologies spans the spectrum of healthcare, with leading businesses and products in diagnostics, medical devices, nutritionals and branded generic medicines. Our 114,000 colleagues serve people in more than 160 countries. Abbott in Ireland Abbott has been operating in Ireland since 1946 and serves the Irish market with a diverse range of healthcare products including diagnostics, diabetes care, medical devices and nutritional products. In Ireland, Abbott currently employs about 6,000 people across ten sites located in Dublin, Donegal, Clonmel, Cootehill, Galway, Kilkenny, Longford and Sligo. Please note any offer of employment for this position is conditional upon the successful completion of a pre-employment medical examination, including drug and alcohol screening, conducted by a medical practitioner designated by the Company. Abbott Diabetes Care Kilkenny Abbott Diabetes Care has opened a new site, in Kilkenny, that is at the forefront of Diabetes care with the latest technology, to manufacture FreeStyle Libre 3 blood glucose testing in diabetes management. Among Abbott Diabetes Care leading brands are the FreeStyle Precision, FreeStyle Lite and FreeStyle Libre systems . MAIN PURPOSE OF ROLE In this role, you will be responsible for delivering immediate operational impact by optimizing the utilization of personnel, processes, and equipment within Operations. You will apply Lean Six Sigma tools to drive near‑term improvements, including process flow development, value stream mapping, time studies, line balancing, and the reduction of material and labour waste. You will update and develop standard work, analyse headcount versus capacity models, and embed Lean and MOS principles into existing facility infrastructure. Through the application of lean fundamentals, you will improve quality, cost, safety, and delivery performance across operations. In parallel, you will build and own a medium to long‑term operational excellence strategy, translating business and growth objectives into a clearly defined roadmap for future manufacturing layouts, capability development, and facility evolution. This strategy will ensure sustainable performance improvements, scalability, and alignment with organisational priorities, while enabling continuous improvement to be embedded across Operations over time. An Industrial Engineer’s role isn’t just to improve metrics—it’s to ensure the system delivers: Faster lead time Higher quality Lower cost Better experience MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES Responsible for identifying and implementing improvements to strategic site operations and processes to support the delivery of site goals. Lead industrial engineering activities relating to clean room and equipment expansion programs and achievement of planned capacity. Apply engineering methods to deliver models and presentations relating to facility, capacity and expansion planning and recommendations to management. Measure and report on efficiency data. Maintain oversight of manufacturing data routinely collected to ensure necessary data is available to inform strategic decision making. Use influencing skills to ensure outcomes of analysis are implemented to good effect. Recommend and develop manufacturing methods and tools that support and promote improvements in the utilisation of people, equipment, processes materials and utilities across all business processes. Establish, set and maintain process standards and the capacity book, measuring performance to plan. Deliver solutions to address unfavourable variances. Through knowledge and Lean Sigma tools identify and deliver improvements to reduce waste and improve process and system performance. Analyse capacity requirements to achieve output, propose labour and equipment resources to achieve business plans. Develop and lead continuous improvement projects, working cross functionally to implement changes that deliver savings across the business. Work closely with Systems and Process Engineers to develop and improve dashboard visualisation to support manufacturing teams. Note: This job description describes the principal and main elements of the job. It is a guide to the nature and main duties of the job as they currently exist but it is not intended as a wholly or comprehensive or permanent representation. Qualifications & Experience Bachelor’s degree in engineering / science. +3 Years Relevant Experience, In-depth knowledge and application of industrial engineering techniques, measurement of OEE, and knowledge of Lean Sigma tools. Black Belt Qualification. Experience with overall equipment effectiveness (OEE) data management, statistical process control (SPC), financial budgets/standards. High degree of technical expertise and familiarity with manufacturing processes. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Proficiency in project management and mathematical modelling. Knowledge of Lean principles, Six Sigma, and other improvement methodologies. Ability to work within a team and as an individual contributor in a fast-paced, changing environment. Strong verbal and written communications with ability to effectively communicate at multiple levels in the organization. Key Skillset Lean and Six Sigma Data Analytics and Statistics Project Management Critical Thinking and RCA Workflow and System Design Strong communication and facilitation End to End Thinking What we Offer Attractive compensation package that includes competitive pay, as well as benefits such as Family health insurance, Excellent pension scheme Life assurance Career Development Fantastic new facility Growing business plus access to many more benefits. Connect with us at www.abbott.com or https://www.ie.abbott/, on LinkedIn at www.linkedin.com/company/abbott-/, on Facebook at www.facebook.com/Abbott and on Twitter @AbbottNews. Abbott is an equal opportunities employer. The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. jobs.title: MBO-stage bij Abbott in Weesp
jobs.description: JOB DESCRIPTION: MBO-stage bij Abbott in Weesp Ben jij op zoek naar een uitdagende stageplek waar je kunt werken aan je vaardigheden en kennis binnen de farmaceutische industrie? Abbott in Weesp biedt een interessante stageplek voor een MBO-student die graag wil leren en zichzelf verder wil ontwikkelen. Wat ga je doen? Als stagiair bij Abbott ga je aan de slag op een van onze afdelingen, afhankelijk van jouw opleiding en interesses. Hier krijg je de kans om praktijkervaring op te doen en te leren van ervaren professionals binnen het vakgebied. Je zult betrokken worden bij verschillende projecten en werkzaamheden, waarbij je de mogelijkheid hebt om jouw kennis toe te passen en nieuwe vaardigheden te ontwikkelen. Wat bieden wij? Abbott biedt een dynamische en uitdagende werkomgeving waar je als stagiair volop kunt groeien en leren. Naast de kans om praktijkervaring op te doen, krijg je bij ons de mogelijkheid om jouw theoretische kennis toe te passen en verder te ontwikkelen. Bovendien kom je terecht in een hecht team van professionals die jou graag willen ondersteunen en begeleiden tijdens jouw stage. Bonus: Wij bieden een salaris van 500 euro per maand! Wie zoeken wij? Wij zijn op zoek naar een enthousiaste 3e/ 4e jaars MBO-student die zichzelf verder wil ontwikkelen binnen de farmaceutische industrie. Je volgt een relevante opleiding, bijvoorbeeld in de richting van laboratoriumtechniek of procestechniek, en hebt een grote interesse in dit vakgebied. Je bent leergierig, proactief en kunt goed samenwerken in een team. Heb je interesse? Ben je geïnteresseerd in deze stageplek bij Abbott in Weesp? Solliciteer direct en wie weet verwelkomen we jou binnenkort als stagiair binnen ons team! The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted.
